transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Good afternoon, First National Bank. My name is Carly. Can I take your full name and date of birth for security?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Yeah hi, um — it's, uh, Marcus Webb. And um, my date of birth is, uh — fifth — no, fifteenth? Fifteenth of, uh, July. Nineteen eighty-eight.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: Thank you Mr. Webb, I've confirmed your identity. What can I help you with?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: So I, uh — I made a transfur? Like I sent money? And uh — I realized straight away like, literally — uh — within like ten minutes that I'd, like, put the wrong — uh — account number in. Like I transposed some digits or something.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I completely understand. How much was the transfer for?

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Uh, six — six hundred and, uh — fifty pounds. Yeah, six-fifty.

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: I can see that payment. Since it went via Faster Payments it's already settled, but I'm raising a formal recall request now. This contacts the receiving bank and formally requests the funds back.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Right, yeah. And like — uh — [inaudible] — what are the chances they, like, give it back?

transcript.speaker: agent
transcript.text: If the account number doesn't match any active account, the funds are returned automatically within forty-eight hours. If there is an account holder, they're legally obligated to return funds received in error. Reference number CAN-2024-8099. You'll receive an email confirmation.

transcript.speaker: customer
transcript.text: Okay brilliant. Um, yeah — like, uh — thank you. That's — that's taken a load off.
